text.title: ai-accessibility-automation-2026 Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ai-accessibility-automation-2026 as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Publishing AI-generated VPAT sections externally without human legal/compliance review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: VPAT drafts are legal documents. Any inaccurate conformance claim exposes the organization to legal risk. DRAFT watermark is mandatory on all AI-generated sections; human sign-off required before any external use.

failure-mode.description: Auto-closing issues based on AI re-scan alone without human confirmation on real assistive technology
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: AI scan confirms the code pattern changed, not that the fix works for actual users. A regression may exist that the scanner cannot detect. Human validation on VoiceOver, NVDA, or JAWS is required before closure.

failure-mode.description: Deploying AI overlay widgets as the ADA Title II compliance strategy
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overlays do not satisfy ADA Title II requirements. They are JavaScript patches that cannot fix semantic HTML issues, keyboard navigation, or focus management. Organizations that rely on overlays remain non-compliant.

failure-mode.description: Agent-generated code fixes for complex ARIA widget patterns (combobox, tree, grid) applied without senior review
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARIA widget patterns require precise role/state/property relationships. AI-generated fixes for these patterns are frequently incorrect and may introduce new accessibility failures.

failure-mode.description: Sending large SPA axe JSON (100KB+) to LLM in a single prompt
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Large single-page apps generate axe reports that exceed LLM context limits. Chunk by page or component before sending; aggregate results after processing.

failure-mode.description: Starting automation without an existing accessibility baseline
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without a baseline, CI gates immediately block all deploys due to the full existing backlog. Establish a baseline first, then gate on regressions only.
